trivia_bullets.text: Opus VIII is a 148-card set released in March 2019 featuring characters from Final Fantasy VII, IX, XIV: Stormblood, XV, and Brave Exvius — one of the first sets to incorporate FFXV cards into the FFTCG lineup. [TradingCardSets.com — Final Fantasy TCG Opus VIII Complete Guide; Square Enix FFTCG official release page]

The Basilisk in Final Fantasy IX appears in Burmecia, the rain-soaked rat-people city ravaged by Brahne's forces in Disc 1. It is weak to Ice and uses Tongue (inflicts Sleep) and Gradual Petrify — a countdown status that turns a character to stone when the timer expires, reinforcing the creature's mythological origin as a beast whose gaze petrifies. [Game8.co — Basilisk Stats and Locations FF9; GameFAQs FF9 Burmecia walkthrough]

As an FFTCG card, Basilisk (8-059C) is a Wind-element Monster costing 5 CP with 9000 power. On entry it deals 4000 damage to a Forward; then it can be sacrificed — put into the Break Zone — to break any damaged Forward outright. This two-stage removal package (chip + execute) on a common Wind Monster made it a notable limited-format pickup in Opus VIII draft. [WebSearch snippet via Game-O-Storus product page; FFTCG Crystarium Opus VIII limited review]

The card art is visually consistent with the FFIX enemy model: broad, quadrupedal green-scaled body, enormous spiraling yellow eyes, antennae, and red tongue — the spiraling eyes are a consistent visual marker of the Basilisk across its appearances in FFIX and FFXII, distinguishing it from other lizard-type monsters in the series. [Final Fantasy Wiki via WebSearch snippet — Basilisk (creature)]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom) — Basilisk (creature), via WebSearch snippet
